source_note: Management & Operations Audit issued by the PA PUC Bureau of Audits, published 2023-03-02. PDF: puc.pa.gov/pcdocs/1775875.pdf (the audit report, distinct from the company implementation plan at 1775874). Findings & recommendations parsed verbatim from the Exhibit I-2 Summary of Recommendations.

recommendations.text: Streamline corporate governance processes to efficiently align with PGW’s current regulatory framework.
Streamline the annual budget approval process.
Establish committee charters for all PFMC board committees.
Evaluate director performance annually to identify opportunities for improvement and to ensure emerging needs and priorities are met.
Develop specific guidance for span of control expectations, periodically review spans of control, and document any narrow or wide spans for PGW’s management positions.
Implement a safety management system and improve safety culture at PGW.
Update Internal Audit policies and procedures.
Formally document explanations for variances from the capital and operating budgets.
Implement a policy and metrics-tracked plan to replace mercury regulators within the distribution system.
Accelerate cast iron main replacement.
Plan and implement a process for gathering equipment and installation data in the GIS database.
Reorganize the CARC reporting structure to eliminate conflicts of interest with the SVP of Operations.
File Self-Certification forms with the Pennsylvania PUC annually.
Correct various deficiencies in physical security.
Establish a company-wide program for inspections for safety, security, medical, and fire equipment, and make assessable standardized first aid kits at all regularly occupied facilities.
Explore ways to eliminate or mitigate the identified security risk through a business case analysis.

Increase focus on cybersecurity by developing a dedicated cybersecurity budget, performing a staffing study and adjusting resources as needed, and create an executive level cybersecurity leadership position.
Reinforce contingencies with the OT systems by cross-training, increasing resources, or by some other method.
Document the threshold where cybersecurity risks will be relayed to the cabinet and board levels.
Implement a cybersecurity-focused risk registry.
Document the policies and procedures of PGW’s cycle counting function.
Implement and/or increase automation of materials management processes to improve efficiency, accuracy, and ease-of-use.
Leverage pandemic and low-income resources to help reduce the overall level of outstanding customer balances and maintain outreach efforts to engage payment troubled customers.
Improve customer service performance through expanding call center resources.
Complete implementation of the replacement CIS.
Establish reporting and key performance metrics for all back-office activities that support electronic, self-service, and alternative exchanges with customers.
Repurpose or divest interest in district offices and reallocate resources to benefit PGW ratepayers.
Establish IS departmental performance metrics for transparency, evaluation, and improvement of productivity and efficiency.
Leverage and integrate data visualization software consistently across PGW.
Improve efficiencies within the fleet department.
Implement strategies for recruitment and retention of “at-risk” positions.
Drive safety performance to meet industry standards.
